description: Figure 5 displays EBSD inverse pole figure (IPF) maps and phase distribution maps for the Fe-35Mn-17Al-5Cu (17Al) low-density steel alloy subjected to different aging treatments. Panels a1 and a2 show the microstructure after aging at 525°C for 10 h, where only 20.5% β-Mn (green) has precipitated along α grain boundaries and within grains, alongside small α-Mn particles (blue). Panels b1 and b2 present the sample aged at 575°C for 6 h, revealing nearly complete transformation of the α matrix to β-Mn with fine new FCC phase precipitates (pink) distributed within the β-Mn. Panels c1 and c2 illustrate the microstructure after aging at 650°C for 10 h, showing complete α-to-β-Mn transformation accompanied by new BCC phase particles (red) within the β-Mn matrix. The magnified views (a2-c2) clearly resolve these secondary phases at interfaces. These micrographs demonstrate that higher Al content slows β-Mn precipitation kinetics compared to the 15Al alloy, while elevated aging temperatures promote the formation of secondary BCC and FCC phases that affect the hardening response.
